jurisdictions.basis_short: Materially influences a 'consequential decision' (credit) under §1701(3); deployer obligations under §§1703–1705 apply.
ADMT under CPPA regs §7001(e); pre-use notice + access right required.
NY ADMT regime (S 7543) not yet effective; LL144 covers employment only.
IL has no general ADMT law; BIPA applies only if biometric data is collected (no signal here).
No state ADMT regime; federal ECOA/Reg B governs.
Could be 'materially influencing' if fraud flag drives an approve/deny outcome rather than queueing for review.
Falls within 'fraud prevention' permitted purpose under CCPA regs.
Out of LL144 scope (not an employment decision tool).
No biometric data implicated.
No state ADMT regime.
Personalized credit-term offers fall within 'price determination' under §1701(3)(b)(II).
Behavioral advertising / personalization in financial services triggers CCPA ADMT pre-use notice.
Out of LL144 scope.
No biometric data implicated.
No state ADMT regime.
AEDT under NYC LL144 §20-870; independent bias audit + pre-use candidate notice required.
ADMT under CPPA regs; pre-use notice + access right required for employment use.
Materially influences a 'consequential decision' (employment) under §1701(3)(b)(IV).
IL AI in Hiring Act requires candidate notice; BIPA does not apply (no biometrics).
No state employment ADMT regime.
AEDT + significant LL144 bias-audit scope. Notice and bias-audit publication required.
IL AI Video Interview Act + BIPA: candidate consent required before recording; biometric storage triggers full BIPA stack.
Materially influences employment decision; CO SB 26-189 deployer obligations apply.
ADMT under CPPA regs; additional CCPA biometric-information rules apply.
TX CUBI applies to biometric retention but no ADMT regime.
AEDT if output substantially assists or replaces discretionary decisions; pre-use notice required.
IL AI in Hiring Act notice if video component used; otherwise general fairness requirements apply.
May 'materially influence' if results gate next-stage selection.
ADMT under CPPA regs for employment use.
No state regime.
ADMT under CPPA regs §7001(e); covered consumer decision (credit). Pre-use notice + access right required. Cross-system telematics use triggers additional disclosure under §7220(c).
Materially influences 'consequential decision' (credit) under §1701(3)(b)(II); §§1703–1705 deployer obligations apply.
No state ADMT regime for credit; BIPA inapplicable (no biometrics in finance flow).
NY ADMT regime (S 7543) not yet effective; LL144 is employment-only.
No state ADMT regime; federal ECOA/Reg B governs.
BIPA: real-time facial biometric processing and any retention beyond the immediate alert require written informed consent and a public retention schedule. 740 ILCS 14/15.
ADMT under CPPA regs; CCPA biometric-information disclosure required. Triggers risk-assessment obligation if used for any decision beyond real-time alerting.
Covered if any output materially influences a consequential decision (e.g., insurance pricing). Pure safety-alert use is edge-case.
TX CUBI applies to biometric capture/retention but does not regulate ADMT directly.
Out of LL144 scope (not an employment decision tool).
CCPA + CPPA ADMT regs: behavioral cohort assignment using sensitive personal information (precise geolocation) triggers pre-use notice and sensitive-data right to limit.
CPA + ADMT: profiling using personal data for targeted advertising requires opt-out.
No state profiling regime; BIPA inapplicable.
Out of LL144 scope.
No state ADMT or profiling regime.
